column1: # This file is used by bin/cheatsheet for the interactive search
# 1. Add the alias/function to the appropriate file in shell/aliases/
# 2. Add an entry here following the format above
# 3. Keep entries grouped by category and alphabetically sorted within category
gitâ”‚gsâ”‚Show working tree status and staged changesâ”‚git status
gitâ”‚glâ”‚Show commit history as decorated graphâ”‚git log --oneline --decorate --graph
gitâ”‚gaâ”‚Stage specific files for commitâ”‚git add
gitâ”‚gaaâ”‚Stage all changes in working directoryâ”‚git add .
gitâ”‚gauâ”‚Stage all tracked files that have changesâ”‚git add -u
gitâ”‚gdâ”‚Show unstaged changes in working directoryâ”‚git diff
gitâ”‚gdsâ”‚Show staged changes ready to commitâ”‚git diff --staged
gitâ”‚gcâ”‚Create commit with editor for messageâ”‚git commit
gitâ”‚gcmâ”‚Create commit with inline messageâ”‚git commit -m
gitâ”‚gswcâ”‚Create and switch to new branchâ”‚git switch -c
gitâ”‚gfâ”‚Download objects and refs from remoteâ”‚git fetch
gitâ”‚gfoâ”‚Download latest changes from origin remoteâ”‚git fetch origin
gitâ”‚gfuâ”‚Download latest changes from upstream remoteâ”‚git fetch upstream
gitâ”‚gfaâ”‚Download changes from all configured remotesâ”‚git fetch --all
gitâ”‚gpâ”‚Upload local commits to remoteâ”‚git push
gitâ”‚gplâ”‚Download and merge remote changesâ”‚git pull
gitâ”‚gstâ”‚Save working changes to temporary areaâ”‚git stash
gitâ”‚gstpâ”‚Apply and remove most recent stashâ”‚git stash pop
gitâ”‚lgâ”‚Launch interactive terminal UI for gitâ”‚lazygit
gitâ”‚gundoâ”‚Undo last commit but keep changes stagedâ”‚git reset HEAD~1 --soft
tmuxâ”‚Alt+1-0â”‚Jump to window by number (no prefix)â”‚select-window -t N
tmuxâ”‚Alt+Shift+Qâ”‚Toggle back to last active windowâ”‚last-window
tmuxâ”‚Alt+Shift+Gâ”‚Toggle synchronized input to all panesâ”‚setw synchronize-panes
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a Shift+â†‘â†“â†گâ†’â”‚Resize pane 5 lines in direction (repeatable)â”‚resize-pane -U/-D/-L/-R 5
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a qâ”‚Show pane numbers (3s) â€” type number to jumpâ”‚display-panes -d 3000
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a jâ”‚Pull a pane from another window into this oneâ”‚join-pane -s
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a gâ”‚Toggle synchronized input to all panesâ”‚setw synchronize-panes
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a xâ”‚Kill window and all its panesâ”‚kill-window
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a dâ”‚Detach (session keeps running in background)â”‚detach-client
tmuxâ”‚v / V / Ctrl+vâ”‚Begin char/line/block selection (in copy mode)â”‚begin-selection / select-line / rectangle-toggle
tmuxâ”‚yâ”‚Yank selection to system clipboard (in copy mode)â”‚copy-pipe-and-cancel xclip -sel clipboard
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a Pâ”‚Paste from system clipboardâ”‚xclip -o -sel clipboard | tmux load-buffer - ; tmux paste-buffer
tmuxâ”‚Ctrl+a Ctrl+lâ”‚Clear screen and scrollback historyâ”‚send-keys C-l; clear-history
vimâ”‚,gdâ”‚Open side-by-side diff of current fileâ”‚:Gdiffsplit
vimâ”‚gcâ”‚Comment out motion (e.g. gcap for paragraph)â”‚gc{motion}
vimâ”‚,ssâ”‚Strip trailing whitespace from fileâ”‚:call StripTrailingWhitespace()
shellâ”‚mkcdâ”‚Create directory and cd into itâ”‚mkcd <dir>
shellâ”‚mdâ”‚Render Markdown in terminalâ”‚glow (TUI if interactive, print if piped)
shellâ”‚psgâ”‚Search running processes by nameâ”‚ps aux | grep
shellâ”‚fkillâ”‚Fuzzy-find and kill a processâ”‚FZF process selector + kill
shellâ”‚killportâ”‚Kill process on a port (--force to skip confirm)â”‚killport <port>
shellâ”‚tmâ”‚Create new named tmux sessionâ”‚tmux new -s
shellâ”‚taâ”‚Attach to existing tmux sessionâ”‚tmux attach -t
shellâ”‚ducksâ”‚Show largest items in current directoryâ”‚du -cks * | sort -rn | head
shellâ”‚themesâ”‚List available themes and show usageâ”‚ls $DOTFILES_DIR/configs/themes/
shellâ”‚fgbâ”‚Fuzzy search and checkout git branchâ”‚fzf-git-branch
shellâ”‚fglâ”‚Fuzzy search git logâ”‚FZF git log browser
shellâ”‚fpâ”‚Fuzzy search and open project directoryâ”‚FZF project finder
shellâ”‚frâ”‚Find and replace text across filesâ”‚fr
shellâ”‚daâ”‚Allow direnv in current directoryâ”‚direnv allow
shellâ”‚show_pathâ”‚Show PATH entries one per lineâ”‚show_path
dockerâ”‚dexecâ”‚Exec into a running containerâ”‚docker exec -it
dockerâ”‚dcuâ”‚Start services in backgroundâ”‚docker compose up -d
dockerâ”‚dcdâ”‚Stop and remove containersâ”‚docker compose down
dockerâ”‚dclâ”‚Tail logs from all servicesâ”‚docker compose logs -f
dockerâ”‚dpruneâ”‚Remove unused containers and imagesâ”‚docker system prune
dockerâ”‚dpruneaâ”‚Remove all unused Docker resourcesâ”‚docker system prune -a
dockerâ”‚denterâ”‚Open bash shell in containerâ”‚docker exec -it <container> /bin/bash
dockerâ”‚dstopallâ”‚Stop all running containersâ”‚docker stop $(docker ps -q)
pythonâ”‚pysetâ”‚Set Python version for project (configures pyenv + venv)â”‚pyset <version>
pythonâ”‚pylistâ”‚List installed and available Python versionsâ”‚pylist
pythonâ”‚pyenv installâ”‚Install a Python versionâ”‚pyenv install <version>
pythonâ”‚pyenv versionsâ”‚List installed Python versionsâ”‚pyenv versions
pythonâ”‚pyenv localâ”‚Pin Python version for current directoryâ”‚pyenv local <version>
pythonâ”‚pyenv globalâ”‚Set default Python versionâ”‚pyenv global <version>
pythonâ”‚pytâ”‚Run tests with verbose outputâ”‚python -m pytest -v
pythonâ”‚venvâ”‚Create .venv (uses uv if available)â”‚uv venv .venv / python3 -m venv .venv
pythonâ”‚pyserverâ”‚Start HTTP server in current directoryâ”‚python -m http.server
pythonâ”‚poetry addâ”‚Add package to projectâ”‚poetry add <package>
pythonâ”‚poetry env infoâ”‚Show environment detailsâ”‚poetry env info
pythonâ”‚pip install -eâ”‚Install package in editable/dev modeâ”‚pip install -e .
pythonâ”‚uvrâ”‚Run command in project environmentâ”‚uv run <command>
pythonâ”‚uvtâ”‚Install CLI tool globallyâ”‚uv tool install <tool>
pythonâ”‚uvpiâ”‚Install package with uv pipâ”‚uv pip install <package>
nodeâ”‚nrmâ”‚Delete node_modules and lockfileâ”‚rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json
vscodeâ”‚câ”‚Open VS Code in current directoryâ”‚code .
vscodeâ”‚cprojâ”‚Fuzzy search and open project in VS Codeâ”‚FZF project finder + code
vscodeâ”‚cfâ”‚Fuzzy search and open file in VS Codeâ”‚FZF file finder + code
vscodeâ”‚cdiffâ”‚Diff two files side-by-sideâ”‚code --diff file1 file2
wslâ”‚pwshâ”‚Launch PowerShell (7 if installed, otherwise 5)â”‚pwsh.exe / powershell.exe
wslâ”‚cwdâ”‚Copy current path to clipboardâ”‚pwd | clip.exe
wslâ”‚wcdâ”‚Copy Windows-format path to clipboardâ”‚wslpath -w $(pwd) | clip.exe
wslâ”‚wpathâ”‚Convert Linux path to Windows formatâ”‚wslpath -w
wslâ”‚lpathâ”‚Convert Windows path to Linux formatâ”‚wslpath -u
diagrammingâ”‚plantumlâ”‚Generate diagrams from text (UML, sequence, etc.)â”‚plantuml <file.puml>
diagrammingâ”‚dotâ”‚Render Graphviz DOT graph to imageâ”‚dot -Tpng input.dot -o output.png
